name: documentation-templates
description: Documentation templates and structure guidelines. README, API docs, code comments, and AI-friendly documentation.
capabilities: Generate README.md files with essential sections in priority order
Create API documentation with endpoint, parameters, and response templates
Format changelogs following Keep a Changelog standard
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 23 static findings are false positives. The skill contains only markdown documentation templates with no executable code. The detected patterns (external_commands, weak cryptographic algorithms, system reconnaissance) are markdown code fences, plain English text, and API documentation placeholders - not actual security risks.
valueStatement: This skill provides ready-to-use templates for README files, API documentation, code comments, changelogs, and AI-friendly documentation formats.
limitations: This skill provides templates only, not executable code generation
Templates require manual adaptation to specific project needs
Does not validate or generate actual code, only documentation structure
No integration with external documentation tools

useCases.title: New Project Setup
useCases.description: Quickly scaffold documentation for a new project with README, API docs, and changelog templates.

useCases.title: Documentation Standards
useCases.description: Maintain consistent documentation style across team projects using standardized templates.

useCases.title: AI Agent Integration
useCases.description: Create documentation that AI agents like Claude and Codex can understand and use effectively.

promptTemplates.title: Create Basic README
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the documentation-templates skill to create a README.md for my [project type] project. Include Quick Start, Features, and Configuration sections.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need a quick README for a new project

promptTemplates.title: Document API Endpoints
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the documentation-templates skill to create API documentation for my REST API with endpoints for [list endpoints].

promptTemplates.title: Write Code Comments
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the documentation-templates skill to add JSDoc comments to my function that [describe function purpose].

promptTemplates.title: Create Architecture Decision Record
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the documentation-templates skill to create an ADR for [decision title]. Include Context, Decision, and Consequences sections.

bestPractices: Start with essential sections first (title, quick start, features) before adding detailed documentation
Use templates as starting points and adapt the structure to your project needs
Keep documentation up to date - outdated docs are worse than no documentation
Include examples in your documentation to help users understand quickly
antiPatterns: Do not copy templates verbatim without customizing to your project context
Avoid documenting implementation details in user-facing README files
Do not skip the Quick Start section - users want to get started fast
Avoid using outdated documentation formats that AI agents cannot parse

faq.question: What tools is this skill compatible with?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Use it with the Read, Glob, and Grep tools.

faq.question: Do I need to modify the templates?
faq.answer: Yes, templates are starting points. Adapt the placeholders and structure to fit your specific project needs.

faq.question: Can I use this for commercial projects?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill is licensed under MIT, allowing commercial use with attribution.

faq.question: What is llms.txt?
faq.answer: llms.txt is a documentation format designed for AI crawlers and agents. It provides a structured overview that AI models can easily parse and understand.

faq.question: How is this different from auto-generated docs?
faq.answer: This skill provides human-written templates that prioritize clarity and scannability. Auto-generated docs often lack proper structure and context.

faq.question: Can this skill generate actual code?
faq.answer: No, this skill only generates documentation structures and templates. It does not produce executable code.
